name: Arduino
description: An open-source electronics platform based on easy-to-use hardware and software. Used extensively in physics labs for building custom sensors, data acquisition systems, and experimental controls.
whyUseIt: Arduino provides a low-cost, programmable way to interface with the physical world — reading sensors, controlling motors, and collecting data. It is perfect for building custom experimental apparatus when commercial equipment is too expensive or inflexible.
fieldPosition: Experimental physics — custom instrumentation, data acquisition, and lab automation
physicistWorkflow: Connect sensors to Arduino, write code to read and process signals, display data in real time, and log to a computer.
supervisorExpects: Ability to build simple data acquisition systems and custom sensors for experimental work.
computationalImpact: Enables custom instrumentation at a fraction of the cost of commercial equipment.

vocabulary.definition: An Arduino program — written in a C/C++ variant
Analogue-to-digital converter — converts continuous voltage to discrete digital values

beginnerMistakes: Not accounting for ADC resolution and noise in measurements
Using analogRead in a tight loop without considering timing
